text:
St Mary's Hospital 

Institute of Neurology 

Clinic Date: 08 April 2022 

Dr Sharma  
St Mary's Hospital  
Institute of Neurology, St Mary's Hospital, London W2 1NY  

Dear Dr Sharma,  

Oliver Bennett, DOB: 12-03-1986, Hospital No: H928451 NHS No. 5938274410  
14 Rowan Court, London, W10 6PT 

Medication: 

Levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily. No adverse effects reported. 

Since her last review, she reports a clear and sustained improvement in day-to-day stability. Notably, she confirms: "No drop attacks since 2/5/2021." She has also had a marked reduction in brief warning sensations and no episodes requiring emergency assistance. 

She attributes much of this improvement to adherence to dietetic guidance, specifically a structured ketogenic approach undertaken with community dietetic support. She has maintained good hydration, regular meal timing, and careful monitoring of ketone levels as advised by her dietitian. Energy levels have been steady and she notes better sleep continuity. 

There have been no new neurological symptoms, no injuries, and no hospital presentations. Family have observed improved alertness and fewer interrupted activities. She continues to avoid identified personal triggers (sleep deprivation and missed meals). She is keeping a seizure and symptom diary, which corroborates the improvement. 

On current assessment there is no indication to adjust medication. We discussed continuing the present regimen alongside the dietetic plan, with attention to adequate micronutrient intake as per dietetic advice. She understands DVLA guidance and will continue to follow safety precautions for bathing and working at heights. 

Plan: 
- Continue levetiracetam 1000 mg twice daily. 
- Maintain ketogenic dietary plan under dietetic supervision. 
- Continue seizure diary and report any breakthrough events. 
- Routine bloods via GP if clinically indicated (including nutritional monitoring per dietetic protocol). 
- Review in six months, or earlier if there is any deterioration. 

Yours sincerely

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: Clinic date is 08 April 2022. The letter states: "No drop attacks since 2/5/2021." Assuming day/month/year format common in UK clinical letters, last drop attack was 02 May 2021. Calculate duration of seizure freedom up to clinic date: from 02 May 2021 to 08 April 2022 is 11 months and 6 days, i.e., at least 11 months. The letter also notes no episodes requiring emergency assistance and overall improvement, with no other seizures described. Therefore, the patient meets the criterion of seizure-free for at least 6 months. Normalize to "seizure free for 11 month" per allowed formats.
